data.title: Child Dies After Accidental Shooting in Hominy
data.description: Osage County officials are investigating after a tragic shooting death of a 3-year-old child in Hominy on Friday. The Hominy Police Department says the shooting occurred just after 11:45 a.m. Friday. Upon arrival, officers and EMS personnel located a child suffering from a gunshot wound. The child, who was with a parent at the time of the incident, was transported by ambulance to the Cleveland Area Hospital and later transferred to St. Francis Hospital in Tulsa. Police say shortly after 3 p.m., the child succumbed to the injuries at the hospital. The Osage County Sheriff’s Office is...

data.author: Andrew J. Polk
data.title: ABC-7 Xtra Sunday – El Paso county’s changing (and slowing) population growth
data.description: EL PASO, Texas (KVIA) -- Population growth in El Paso county is slowing down. Previously, the area had seen double-digit percent increases in population every decade since the 1950s. But the 2020 census showed that growth cut in half over ten years, falling from 17.8% to 8.1 %. While the city of El Paso and surrounding communities are still growing now, ElThe post ABC-7 Xtra Sunday – El Paso county’s changing (and slowing) population growth appeared first on KVIA.

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander visits Australian War Memorial [Image 17 of 17]
data.description: U.S. and Australian service members, and Matt Anderson, center left, director, Australian War Memorial, observe monuments at The Australian War Memorial in Canberra, Australia, Nov. 6, 2025. U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, toured the memorial and took part in a wreath-laying ceremony honoring the shared sacrifices of U.S. and Australian service members through more than a century of partnership. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander visits Australian War Memorial [Image 16 of 17]
data.description: U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, left, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, interacts with Australian Army Brigadier Dean Thompson, G3, Joint Force Land Component Commander, in Canberra, Australia, Nov. 5, 2025. The visit, which included briefs and office calls, reaffirmed a century of U.S.–Australian defense cooperation and underscored ongoing efforts to enhance interoperability and uphold a free and open Indo-Pacific. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander visits Australian War Memorial [Image 12 of 17]
data.description: U.S. and Australian service members pose for a photo at The Australian War Memorial in Canberra, Australia, Nov. 6, 2025. U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, toured the memorial and took part in a wreath-laying ceremony honoring the shared sacrifices of U.S. and Australian service members through more than a century of partnership. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander visits Australian War Memorial [Image 13 of 17]
data.description: U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, left, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, speaks with Matt Anderson, director, Australian War Memorial, in Canberra, Australia, Nov. 6, 2025. Glynn toured the memorial and took part in a wreath-laying ceremony honoring the shared sacrifices of U.S. and Australian service members through more than a century of partnership. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander visits Australian War Memorial [Image 15 of 17]
data.description: Australian Army Col. Jo Richards, right, director of international engagements, and Brigadier Dean Thompson, center, G3, Joint Force Land Component Commander, greet U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, in Canberra, Australia, Nov. 5, 2025. The visit, which included briefs and office calls, reaffirmed a century of U.S.–Australian defense cooperation and underscored ongoing efforts to enhance interoperability and uphold a free and open Indo-Pacific. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.title: MARFORPAC Commander attends Sea Power Conference [Image 13 of 14]
data.description: U.S. Marine Corps Lt. Gen. James F. Glynn, right, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, and Maj. Bradley Coler, center, Oceania and South Asia desk officer, Plans Division, G-5, MARFORPAC, speak with Simon Butler, chairman, Australian Standardised Shipyard Infrastructure Strategy Maritime, during the Indo-Pacific Sea Power Conference 2025 at the International Convention Centre in Sydney, Australia, Nov. 4, 2025. Service members, defense officials, and industry leaders gather for the conference, co-hosted by the Royal Australian Navy, to strengthen regional partnerships and advance...

data.title: MARFORPAC Commander attends Sea Power Conference [Image 12 of 14]

Glynn, left, commander, U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Pacific, speaks with Australian Standardised Shipyard Infrastructure Strategy Maritime representatives during the Indo-Pacific Sea Power Conference 2025 at the International Convention Centre in Sydney, Australia, Nov. 4, 2025. Service members, defense officials, and industry leaders gather for the conference, co-hosted by the Royal Australian Navy, to strengthen regional partnerships and advance maritime security. The event highlights this year’s theme, “Strength at Sea = Security and Prosperity at Home...

data.author: Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor
data.title: MARFORPAC Commander attends Sea Power Conference [Image 11 of 14]
data.description: Model technologies are displayed during the Indo-Pacific Sea Power Conference 2025 at the International Convention Centre in Sydney, Australia, Nov. 4, 2025. Service members, defense officials, and industry leaders gather for the conference, co-hosted by the Royal Australian Navy, to strengthen regional partnerships and advance maritime security. The event highlights this year’s theme, “Strength at Sea = Security and Prosperity at Home,” reflecting Australia’s enduring reliance on the sea for stability and growth. (U.S. Marine Corps photo by Lance Cpl. Jose Villasenor)

data.author: Gena Tripp
data.title: Perry graduate says he’s barred from hosting alumni events after park damage dispute
data.description: PERRY, Georgia (41NBC/WMGT) – A Perry High School graduate says he’s been prohibited from hosting alumni gatherings on city property after the City of Perry cited rule violations and damage following an alumni tailgate at Heritage Oaks Park. The decision has sparked confusion and frustration among some Perry High School alumni online, where the issue first gained attention on Facebook....
